query: What must LFI do when credit risk increases?
answer:
1. Determine if credit risk has increased significantly (“SICR”) using all available, reasonable and supportable information—including forward-looking information—against the LFI’s internal SICR policy and the accounting standards cited in the policy [4].
2. Once SICR is identified, stage the obligor/facility in accordance with Article 9 on classification and provisioning [4].
3. Re-assess the obligor using up-to-date metrics (probability of default, loss given default, exposure at default, and updated collateral values) and at the required granularity [5].
4. Implement risk-mitigating and corrective actions appropriate to the increased risk profile; examples include requesting additional collateral, downgrading ratings, restructuring the credit facility, liquidating or selling the exposure, hedging, portfolio rebalancing or other measures, and document the rationale and steps taken [2].
5. Ensure the risk limits, policies and processes remain aligned with the Board-approved Risk Appetite statement, the LFI’s Risk Profile and its capital strength [3, 6].
6. Communicate updated risk limits and relevant information to staff as required [3].

Uncertainty: None – all obligations are explicitly covered by the cited clauses.

evidence.title: The Board must oversee and ensure that Senior Management is fully capable of managing
evidence.text:
the Credit Risk bearing activities conducted by the LFI and that such activities are within the
Risk Appetite, strategy, policies and tolerances reviewed and approved by the Board. In
addition, the Board must oversee and ensure that policies and processes are implemented
effectively and fully integrated into the LFI’s overall risk management process.

evidence.title: Risk mitigation: Upon the review of the performance of individual credit instruments and
evidence.text:
portfolios, LFIs must have a robust process to discuss observations, escalate concerns and
implement risk mitigating actions and corrective actions, such as an additional collateral
request, a rating downgrade, Credit Facility restructuring, liquidation, sell -off, hedging,
portfolio rebalancing, or any other action. Such process must be rigorously documented.

evidence.title: Risk Limits: LFIs must implement prudent and appropriate credit limits based on adequate
evidence.text:
metrics and at a suitable level of segmentation granularity. These limits must be consistent
with the Board -approved Risk Appetite statement, Risk Profile and capital strength. Risk
Limits should be understood by, and regularly communicated to, relevant staff.

evidence.title: The LFI must assess and document regularly whether the Credit Risk of a financial
evidence.text:
instrument has increased significantly since its initial recognition. The identification of
Significant Increase in Credit Risk (“SICR”) must be done in accordance with
accounting standards, the LFI’s internal policy on SICR and must incorporate the
requirements presented in these standards. It must be based on all available, reasonable
and supportable information; including forward-looking information. The identification
of SICR must be employed by LFIs to determine the staging of Obligors and Facilities as
explained in Article 9 below on classification and provisioning.

evidence.title: Analytics: LFI must implement adequate methodologies, models and analytical tools to
evidence.text:
identify and measure Credit Risk regularly at several levels of aggregation and segmentation.
This assessment must rely on Obligors’ probability of Default, loss given Default, the size
of their Credit Facility upon Default and the estimation of collateral values.

evidence.title: The Board must regularly review, and approve, the Credit Risk management strategy,
evidence.text:
framework, significant policies, tolerances/limits and processes for identifying, measuring,
evaluating, monitoring, reporting and controlling or mitigating Credit Risk. The Board must
also ensure these are consistent with the Risk Appetite and capital strength and other relevant
parameters that they set. At a minimum, it must approve the organisation of the LFI’s Credit
Risk Management Functions, including the credit underwriting process, the independent
review of such underwriting, the portfolio management of credit exposures and the
management of distressed assets.

reasoning_path: Retrieved 6 clause(s) by semantic similarity across the indexed corpus.
Evidence maps to 5 canonical concept(s): Credit Risk, Credit Risk Appetite and Limits, Credit Risk Measurement and Models, Credit Risk Monitoring, Significant Increase in Credit Risk (SICR).
Those concepts sit under parent concept(s): Credit Risk.
Found 2 related clause(s) in other documents sharing these concepts (cross-reference candidates).
5 of 6 evidence clause(s) rely on a concept mapping still pending compliance review.

notes: 5 evidence clause(s) use draft concept mappings not yet compliance-approved.
LLM cited 1 clause(s) not in the evidence set: [3, 6] — treat as ungrounded.
